message:
fix(fuzz): fix SSE parser fuzz target chunking to split at valid char boundaries

fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_sse_parser.rs:
- Strategy 3 (two-chunk split) was splitting raw bytes at data.len()/2,
  then converting each half independently via from_utf8_lossy. This
  produced different replacement characters at split points vs the
  whole-input parse, causing spurious chunking invariant violations
  that were not real parser bugs.
- Fix: convert to string once via from_utf8_lossy, then split the
  resulting string at a valid char boundary (scanning forward from
  midpoint). This ensures both halves are valid UTF-8 substrings of
  the same input the whole-input parser sees, making the chunking
  equivalence assertion meaningful.

tests/fuzz_regression.rs (new):
- Regression test harness that replays all committed crash and
  regression corpus inputs through the same SSE parser chunking
  invariant assertions used by the fuzz target
- Reads from fuzz/crashes/ and fuzz/regression/ directories, asserting
  that all stored inputs satisfy the whole/char-by-char/split
  equivalence property
- Serves as a CI-friendly guard against regressions in previously
  discovered crash inputs

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
